creator: wolfgang@lyxys.ka.sub.org
text:
When I try to run a 32bit windows program on wine, I get the message

wine [wine-6.0.2] and wine64 [wine-6.0.3] versions do not match!
Try updating 32-bit wine with /usr/local/share/wine/pkg32.sh upgrade

Calling /usr/local/share/wine/pkg32.sh upgrade results in a message that all packages are up to date though.

I guess the packages repository is simply not up to date yet and I will have to wait. Is there a way to update the wine version myself? I have built emulators/wine from ports, so apparently that is not sufficient to build a wine binary for running 32bit programs.
